EXP-1531–1538: Physics-model fidelity as primary therapy assessment

Replace ADA consensus-based grading (A/B/C/D) with physics-model
fidelity metrics that measure how well therapy settings match glucose
dynamics science, rather than judging patient outcomes against
population targets. Outcomes are a patient's choice; we measure
therapy calibration fidelity.

Key findings across 11 patients:
- RMSE (5.0–16.0 mg/dL) and correction energy are the actionable
  fidelity signals; R² is universally negative (expected with 76.5%
  unannounced meals)
- Correction energy correlates r=0.94 with RMSE but only r=-0.59
  with TIR — fidelity measures something different from outcomes
- 3/11 patients are discordant: ADA grades higher than calibration
  quality warrants (AID loop compensates for poor settings)
- Postprandial R² worse than fasting for all patients — carb ratio
  is the dominant error source
- ADA retained as safety floor only (TBR alerts)

Fidelity grade thresholds calibrated from empirical distribution:
Excellent (RMSE≤6, CE≤600), Good (≤9, ≤1000), Acceptable (≤11,
≤1600), Poor (>11, >1600).

Includes 4 visualization figures and detailed report.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: moderation review queue improvements

- Backend: explicit approval clears stale AdminNotes (null on PUBLISHED,

  verifying DB-level null after approval
- Backend: add ReviewEvent_Unauthenticated_ReturnsAuthError integration test
- Frontend AdminView: inline rejection panel with free-text notes, predefined
  category chips (Incomplete info, Weak description, Invalid URL, Wrong
  category, Past event, Duplicate), cancel/submit actions

  timezone/domain) shown as chips on PENDING_APPROVAL rows

  matching backend semantics

chore: trigger Railway redeploy - benefit engine enabled

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Generate Unicode category/script tables at runtime; update case fold to Unicode 17.0 (#126)

Replace ~2,000 lines of static Unicode category and script arrays with
runtime generation from JDK's Character.getType() and
Character.UnicodeScript.of(). This ensures categories and scripts always
match the running JDK's Unicode version.

Case folding (CASE_FOLD, TO_LOWER) remains static but is updated to
Unicode 17.0 (CaseFolding.txt), adding entries for Garay, Beria Erfe,
Cyrillic TJE, and several Latin pairs. Static fold tables can safely be
ahead of the JDK — entries for unrecognized code points are harmless
dead code, and Unicode's stability policy prevents reassignment.

Also fixes unicodeSpace() which incorrectly included U+001C-001F (file/
group/record/unit separators) — these are not in Unicode's White_Space
property. Filed as #125.

Adds UnicodeJdkConsistencyTest with canary tests that will fail when a
new JDK ships with a newer Unicode version that adds case pairs or
whitespace characters, signaling that the static tables need updating.

Fixes #107
Refs #125

Co-authored-by: Copilot <copilot@github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix all PRs and security: repo-relative links, wiki navigation, workflow hardening, and action security fixes

* Add AI skill sync infrastructure with governance layer

- Canonical project spec (spec v1.0) with TrustSignal-specific rules
- Allowed/forbidden paths, decision rights, approval matrix
- Context drift controls with version discipline
- Model adapters for Claude, OpenAI, Gemini
- Agent policies (primary decision authority + executor bounds)
- GitHub Action workflow for manual validation and adapter refresh

This establishes the source-of-truth control layer for all AI-assisted
development in the repo. All models should reference .ai/skills/project-spec.md
and their model-specific adapter when working on TrustSignal code.

Spec-Version: 1.0

* Fix: Remove unused spawn import, convert absolute paths to relative, sanitize benchmark host/port info

- Remove .worktrees/integration-merge orphaned gitlink

* Fix: Explicitly configure CodeQL languages to bypass failing Swift analysis

A single Swift file in vantademo/public/code4.swift was triggering CodeQL's auto-detection of Swift, which failed due to a lack of Xcode project structure. This change explicitly targets javascript-typescript and python.

commit.message:
fix: correct 8 accuracy issues in hypoglycemia report and figures

Report text fixes:
- Prevalence range: 2.5-10.7% → 0.8-10.7% (Level 1), 0.2-4.1% → 0.0-4.1% (Level 2) per EXP-1493/1491
- Error multiplier: 2.54× → 1.9× (39.8/21.5 from table in context)
- Nadir correlation: mislabeled as IOB-nadir, corrected to recovery-nadir (EXP-1498 field: recovery_vs_nadir_correlation)
- EXP-1281 citation: removed incorrect 33-96% suspension claim (EXP-1281 is basal adequacy, not suspension rates)
- Moderate tier TBR: 0.8-1.0% → 0.75-1.04% (patient d = 0.75%)

Visualization fixes:
- Fig 3 Panel B: carb_treated array corrected for 5/11 patients (c,d,g,h,k were scrambled)
- Fig 1 Panel B: Below 70 R² corrected from 0.153 to 0.149
- Fig 5: hypo_density normalized to sum to 1.0

Regenerated all 6 figures.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Suppress SonarCloud S2254 false positive on getRequestedSessionId() null-check (#1514)

* Initial plan

* fix: suppress SonarCloud S2254 false positive on getRequestedSessionId() in HttpMethodGuardFilter

Add // NOSONAR java:S2254 inline comment to blockRequest() logging line.
The session ID value is never exposed — only null/non-null is checked,
and only "present"/"none" strings are logged.

Fixes #260

commit.message:
Add KQL entity name sanitization and query/command validation (#149)

Entity name sanitization:
- kql_escape_entity_name(): validates and wraps entity names in ['...']
- Accepts properly escaped (['name'], ["name"]) or plain names
- Rejects names with embedded KQL escape sequences to prevent
  query manipulation (e.g., appending sub-queries via entity names)
- kql_escape_string(): escapes single quotes in KQL string literals
- Applied to all entity name interpolation sites

Query/command endpoint validation:
- kusto_query() rejects management commands (starting with '.')
- kusto_command() rejects KQL queries (not starting with '.')
- Prevents accidentally sending queries to the wrong endpoint

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: draft report on hypoglycemia from a data-first perspective

Synthesizes findings from ~1,500 experiments across 11 AID patients on
what hypoglycemic events are, why they are harder to predict than
hyperglycemia, and what the counter-regulatory response looks like from
CGM/pump data alone.

Includes:
- 730-line draft report with 15 explicit assumptions for expert review
- 6 generated figures (anatomy of event, prediction ceiling, TIR-TBR
  paradox, nocturnal timing, observable vs unobservable factors)
- Reproducible figure generation script (tools/generate_hypo_report_figures.py)

Key findings documented:
- 2h HYPO AUC 0.860 (deployable) vs overnight ceiling ~0.69
- Counter-regulatory +5.1 mg/dL systematic bias below 70 mg/dL
- AID-induced hypos account for up to 66.9% of events (patient f)
- TIR-TBR paradox: patient k has 95% TIR but 4.87% TBR
- Two recovery phenotypes: active treaters vs passive reliers

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Initial commit

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Rewrite spec.org to align with corrected architecture

Major changes:
- Remove invented concepts: directed program graphs, IR, MSBuild targets
  for graph validation, three-tier binding, hot-swap AssemblyLoadContext,
  NuGet artifact packaging as core arch, CLI as core arch, 7+ diagnostics
- Add separate TInput/TOutput design: plain C# record inputs, [LmpSignature]
  partial record outputs, Predictor<TIn,TOut> binds them
- Add DSPy-grounded modules: ChainOfThought, BestOfN, Refine, ReActAgent,
  ProgramOfThought (post-MVP), BootstrapFewShot, BootstrapRandomSearch,
  MIPROv2 (post-MVP), Evaluator
- Add actual prompt format: source-gen PromptBuilder<TIn,TOut> creates
  ChatMessage[] with [[ ## field ## ]] delimiters, GetResponseAsync<TOut>
- Update platform primitives: .NET 10 / C# 14 / Aspire GA, IChatClient only
- Move ML.NET, Infer.NET, Z3 to post-MVP extensions table
- Reframe from framework to building blocks that compile
- Source gen focus: PromptBuilder, JsonTypeInfo, GetPredictors, CoT extended
  output record, 2-3 diagnostics (LMP001-LMP003)
- Keep all [1]-[11] business case citations with URLs
- Keep platform primitive map (Section 2A), updated for new architecture
- Document intentionally excluded concepts (Section 22)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Charts: Fix Line Chart Annotations Custom story error in Storybook (#47990)

* Charts: Replace gridicons with inline SVG to fix Storybook Vite transform error

The gridicons package is CJS-only, which causes Vite's CJS-to-ESM interop
in Storybook to fail when resolving the default export. This results in
Gridicon being treated as an object instead of a React component, throwing
'Element type is invalid... got: object' in LineChartAnnotationLabelWithPopover.

Replace the single Gridicon usage (cross icon in popover close button) with
an inline SVG component using the same path data. This eliminates the
CJS interop issue and also removes the gridicons dependency (~106KB) from
the charts package.

Ref CHARTS-208

* Add changelog entry for CHARTS-208 fix

* Charts: Use @wordpress/icons instead of inline SVG for close icon

Replace the inline CloseIcon SVG with the close icon from @wordpress/icons
to align with WordPress ecosystem conventions. The package is tree-shakeable
and already used widely across the monorepo.

* Update changelog entry to reflect @wordpress/icons usage

* Update changelog

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Rewrite source generator spec to focus on emitted artifacts

Refocus the spec on the five things the generator actually emits:

1. PromptBuilder<TInput, TOutput> — assembles ChatMessage[] from
   instructions, input/output field metadata, demos, and current input.
   Shows the actual prompt format (system, demo pairs, current input).

2. JsonTypeInfo<TOutput> — source-generated JSON serialization context
   for zero-reflection AOT-safe serialization.

3. GetPredictors() on LmpModule — returns all Predictor<,> fields
   without runtime reflection (replaces DSPy named_predictors()).

4. ChainOfThought extended output — internal record adding Reasoning
   field to TOutput when ChainOfThought<TIn,TOut> is used.

5. Diagnostics (LMP001-LMP003 only):
   - LMP001: Output property missing [Description]
   - LMP002: Non-JSON-serializable output property
   - LMP003: [LmpSignature] on non-partial record

Dropped: graph IR, program/step/edge descriptors, binding generators,
MSBuild tasks, DI registration helpers, 7-diagnostic catalog.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

response.setCharacterEncoding("UTF-8") before writing JSON to
HTTP responses in 9 action classes. Also fix two instances of
text/javascript → application/json in DocumentPreview2Action,
and add text/plain Content-Type for plain-text ID responses in

commit.message:
Simplify artifact format spec to match DSPy save/load

Replace the over-engineered artifact format (CompiledArtifact, XxHash128,
SHA-256, AssemblyLoadContext hot-swap, NuGet packaging, polymorphic
StepParameter types, ZIP archives) with a flat JSON format that mirrors
DSPy's module.save()/module.load() pattern.

New format: {version, module, predictors: {name: {instructions, demos,
config}}} — one entry per predictor with instructions, few-shot demos,
and optional LM config. SaveAsync/LoadAsync live directly on LmpModule.

Dropped: variantId, baseProgramHash, validationMetrics, approved,
provenance, bindingMetadata, ICompiledArtifactLoader interface,
FileSystemArtifactLoader, HotSwapArtifactProvider,
CollectibleArtifactContext, ArtifactHashing, .lmpa bundle format.

Post-MVP extensions (NuGet packaging, version hashing, hot reload,
metrics sidecar) are noted briefly but not designed in detail.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

sec: suppress 36 Semgrep CRLF log injection false positives in ManageDocument2Action (#1496)

* Initial plan

* sec: add nosemgrep CRLF log injection suppression to ManageDocument2Action

Add // nosemgrep: crlf-injection-logs-deepsemgrep, crlf-injection-logs
inline comments to all 36 log lines in ManageDocument2Action.java that
use LogSanitizer.sanitize(). These are false positives because
Semgrep's taint analysis does not recognize LogSanitizer.sanitize()
(which calls Encode.forJava() internally) as a CRLF sanitizer.

No code logic changes — all LogSanitizer.sanitize() calls remain intact.

Fixes #1432
